cards.back: The practice of protecting sensitive information from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction.
The right of individuals to control how their personal data is collected, used, and shared, and to have access to and control over their data.
A method of converting plaintext into unreadable ciphertext to protect data from unauthorized access.

cards.front: What is a secure socket layer (SSL) certificate?
cards.back: A digital certificate issued by a trusted third-party authority, used to secure online communications and verify the identity of a website.
A network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predetermined security rules to prevent unauthorized access.
